name: structlog
what_it_does: Structured logging library for Python with a processor pipeline architecture. Structlog wraps any logging backend (stdlib, Twisted, etc.) and adds immutable context binding, processor chains for transforming log events, and flexible output formats (JSON, key=value, colored dev output). Designed for production use where logs are machine-parseable and shipped to log aggregators.
use_cases: Emit structured JSON logs from Python services consumable by ELK, Datadog, Loki, or Splunk without post-parsing
Bind request-scoped context (request_id, user_id, trace_id) to all logs within a request handler
Build custom log processor pipelines to add timestamps, filter sensitive fields, or transform log records
Integrate with standard library logging to add structlog's structured output to existing applications
Use async-compatible logging in FastAPI/asyncio applications with contextvars-based context binding
not_for: Quick scripts or development logging — Loguru's zero-config colored output is better for simple use cases
Teams unfamiliar with processor pipeline concepts — structlog has a steeper learning curve than Loguru
Projects with no need for structured output — stdlib logging with a formatter is simpler for unstructured text logs
best_when: You're building production Python services where logs are machine-parsed, you need request-scoped context binding, and you want a configurable processor pipeline.
avoid_when: You want zero-config colorized logging for development scripts — use Loguru.

pricing.notes: Free and open source, maintained by Hynek Schlawack.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: structlog errors are Python exceptions from processor misconfiguration. Well-documented exception types. Development mode provides colored output with detailed context for debugging.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Logging is idempotent by design. Bound context is immutable — bind() returns new logger instance.
known_agent_gotchas: structlog requires explicit configuration via structlog.configure() — without it, output uses development defaults which may not be JSON for production
Context binding with structlog.contextvars.bind_contextvars() uses Python contextvars — this is async-safe but must be cleared per-request with clear_contextvars() to prevent context leakage
The processor chain is ordered — timestamp processor must come before JSON renderer or timestamps won't appear in output
structlog.stdlib.BoundLogger wraps stdlib logging — log levels must match stdlib level names (WARNING not WARN) when using stdlib backend
structlog is immutable — logger.bind(key=val) returns a new logger; the original logger is unchanged; forgetting to use the return value loses context
Stdlib integration requires both structlog.configure() AND logging.basicConfig() or dictConfig — structlog alone doesn't capture stdlib logging without explicit bridging setup

security_score_components.security_notes: Add a filter processor to redact sensitive fields before JSON output. Processor pipeline allows custom security filtering before log emission. No built-in PII redaction — must be implemented as custom processor.
